characters.name: Big 1 (Nightmare Troubadour)
characters.description: Big 1English nameBig 1Japanese translatedBig 1Japanese nameビッグ１GenderMaleNightmare TroubadourOcean ConquererVideo game appearancesYu-Gi-Oh! Nightmare Troubadour This is the depiction of Big 1 fromYu-Gi-Oh! Nightmare Troubadour. He is the first of an 8-duel Boss battle that begins after the Beginners Cup. In the dialogue he still refers to himself by his actual name, Gansley, but the game itself simply refers to him by his alias. The Player duels him in a Shadow Game and wins, causing him to presumably get sent to theShadow Realm. His deck is overall similar in theme to his anime deck.

characters.name: Officer 227
characters.description: Officer 227English nameOfficer 227Japanese translatedDuel Chaser 227Other namesDC227[1][2]Duel Chasers Number 227[1]Japanese nameデュエルチェイサー２２７GenderMaleOccupationDuel Chaser[3]OrganizationSector Security[3]TournamentPositionFriendship CupDid not placeAnime DeckGoyoAnime debutYu-Gi-Oh! ARC-Vepisode 54: "City 'Scape"Anime appearancesYu-Gi-Oh! ARC-VJapanese voiceHiroki Gotō[3] Officer 227, known asDuel Chaser 227in the Japanese version, is a character in theYu-Gi-Oh! ARC-Vanime. He used to work forSector Securityin theSynchro Dimension, but he was demoted and fired for his failure to apprehendYugofor trespassing in theTopsidersarea ofNew Domino City.[3] 227 without his helmet or sunglasses. 227 is confident in his skills, claiming he has a 100% arrest rate and that he wouldn't letYugoget past him.[4]This self-confidence was shown to turn into overconfidence when he thought he could defeat Yugo with a finaldirect attack, ignoringJean-Michel Roget's warning about Yugo'sSetcard, resulting in his loss. He, like manySynchro Dimensionresidents, is heavily competitive on the social scale. After being demoted to theCommons, 227 was shown to have become desperate to regain his former privileges, praising Roget like a savior for the "second chance" of being allowed to enter theFriendship Cup, despite Roget being the one who demoted him in the first place.

He, like manySynchro Dimensionresidents, is heavily competitive on the social scale. After being demoted to theCommons, 227 was shown to have become desperate to regain his former privileges, praising Roget like a savior for the "second chance" of being allowed to enter theFriendship Cup, despite Roget being the one who demoted him in the first place.

characters.name: Abe the Monkey Man
characters.description: Abe the Monkey ManEnglish nameAbe the Monkey ManJapanese translatedApeJapanese nameエイプGenderMaleTournamentPositionGrand ChampionshipTop 8Anime debutYu-Gi-Oh!episode 186: "Unwanted Guest - Part 2"Anime appearancesYu-Gi-Oh!(second series)English voiceAdam BlausteinJapanese voiceMikio Katsuyama Abe the Monkey Manis a monkey-like person who has a pet monkey with him on his shoulder. He Dueled in the Kaiba CorpGrand Championship, containing many of the best Duelists around the world. He beatsTotani Ialosin Round 1 but loses toRebecca Hawkinsin Round 2. According to Abe, his pet monkey is "the smart one", as it seems to make most of the decisions in Duels, selecting which cards to use. No cards in this Duelist's Deck were shown.

characters.name: Abyss (character)
characters.description: AbyssCorresponding cardNumber 73: Abyss SplashNumber C73: Abyss Supra SplashEnglish nameAbyssJapanese nameアビスGenderMaleAnime DeckGorgonicAnime debutYu-Gi-Oh! ZEXALepisode 108: "A Sea of Troubles, Part 1"Anime appearancesYu-Gi-Oh! ZEXALEnglish voiceDavid Zen Mansley[1]Japanese voiceKatsuhiko Sasaki Abyssis a character in theYu-Gi-Oh! ZEXALanime, being a mighty sea god and the spirit form of "Number 73: Abyss Splash" in the service of his master: theBarianEmperorNash. He would first appear as the finalNumber's Guardianentrusted with twoMythyrian"Numbers" forReginald Kastleto inherit after a Duel within an underwater temple to reawaken his original memories. Abyss is fittingly similar to his card form, without the helmet. He has sky blue skin, light green eyes, and wears blue armor slashed with gold and adorned with aquamarine-colored stones. He also wears a gold headpiece on his forehead set with the same aquamarine-colored stone and has black hair and beard arranged in a similar way toShark's hairstyle.

characters.name: Adam (manga)
characters.description: AdamNamesEnglishAdamPersonalGenderMaleAppearancesManga debutYu-Gi-Oh! ARC-VScale 32: "What Adam Saw!!"MangaYu-Gi-Oh! ARC-V Adamis a character appearing in theYu-Gi-Oh! ARC-Vmanga. Adam was a colleague to Eve, who were joined by Isaac in Solid Vision research[1]as they were fleeing from war. Due to the raging war, Adam and Eve were refugees and fled, eventually enlisting into Isaac's university as researchers. The two were close, and wanted to help people through scientific methods, and motivated Isaac to become like them. The trio researched Leo Akaba and Yusho Sakaki's Solid Vision, hoping their research could bring peace to the world, by placing mass onto a single card. They discovered a portal to another world, which could warp them even through a different time. The trio tried to control that energy, so Adam volunteered to become the host of that energy. In the moment Adam grasped the "forbidden fruit" - the G.O.D. card - he saw the humanity's history through various places. Adam became overwhelmed by that experience, and exclaimed the energy in the card he was holding had a will of its own. Eve wanted to stop the experiment, but Adam continued on. The energy of G.O.D. card started warping Adam away, and Isaac, despite being able to save Adam, did nothing. Adam merged with the energy and disappeared. Eve grabbed the G.O.D. card and cried, swearing to use its power to find Adam.[2]In that time, Adam saw an enormous man-made gate, beyond which Adam saw despair - destroyed stars, lifeless planets, forms void of life. Adam believed G.O.D. destroyed these planets.[1]It was why he sealed away some of G.O.D.'s power before disappearing.[3]

In the moment Adam grasped the "forbidden fruit" - the G.O.D. card - he saw the humanity's history through various places. Adam became overwhelmed by that experience, and exclaimed the energy in the card he was holding had a will of its own. Eve wanted to stop the experiment, but Adam continued on. The energy of G.O.D. card started warping Adam away, and Isaac, despite being able to save Adam, did nothing. Adam merged with the energy and disappeared. Eve grabbed the G.O.D. card and cried, swearing to use its power to find Adam.[2]In that time, Adam saw an enormous man-made gate, beyond which Adam saw despair - destroyed stars, lifeless planets, forms void of life. Adam believed G.O.D. destroyed these planets.[1]It was why he sealed away some of G.O.D.'s power before disappearing.[3]

characters.name: Aero
characters.description: AeroEnglish nameAeroJapanese translatedYujiJapanese nameユージGenderMaleAnime debutYu-Gi-Oh! 5D'sepisode 11: "The Take Back, Part 1"Anime appearancesYu-Gi-Oh! 5D'sEnglish voiceAnthony Salerno[citation needed]Japanese voiceShinya Iida[1] Aero, known asYuji(ユージYūji) in the Japanese version, was a friend ofBlister, who was injured in a tag-teamTurbo Duelaccident. Blister was in a tag-team Turbo Duel, with Aero in the side-car. Their opponent spun out and knocked Blister off track, causing the cable connecting the two cars to break. Blister tried to save Aero, but pulled away to avoid crashing. Aero crashed into a factory, while Blister spun away, breaking hisDuel Runner. Unbeknownst to Blister, Aero saved "Machina Sniper", which was their favorite card, from a blaze, causing him to get hit by an explosion which could have been avoided otherwise.[1]
